name: SharpDPAPI
summary: SharpDPAPI is a C# port of some DPAPI functionality from @gentilkiwi's Mimikatz project.

documentation.readme: SharpDPAPI is a C# port of some DPAPI functionality from @gentilkiwi's Mimikatz project.

arguments.name: --depth
arguments.description: CLI flag '--depth' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: --no-color
arguments.description: Disable ANSI color in output.

arguments.name: --quiet
arguments.description: Suppress non-error output.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation prompts (use with care).

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --no-banner
arguments.description: Suppress banner / tool-name output.

arguments.name: --capture
arguments.description: Capture mode: write packets to a pcap file.

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

function_signatures.name: validate
function_signatures.description: Pre-flight check: confirm the target is reachable and the tool can run.

Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with GhostPack/SharpHound (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with wiliam227user/CVE-2018-12633-TPLink-Auth-Bypass (score=9); pair in the chain planner.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'post_exploit' phase against surfaces [shell_windows, shell_linux, ad] in the Post-Exploitation category. Fits when the engagement needs: SharpDPAPI is a C# port of some DPAPI functionality from @gentilkiwi's Mimikatz project.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: SharpDPAPI is a C# port of some DPAPI functionality from @gentilkiwi's Mimikatz project.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `SharpDPAPI.exe search /type:registry [/path:HKLM\path\to\key] [/showErrors]`. Further invocations from upstream: `SharpDPAPI.exe search /type:folder /path:C:\path\to\folder [/maxBytes:<numOfBytes>] [/showErrors]`; `SharpDPAPI.exe search /type:file /path:C:\path\to\file [/maxBytes:<numOfBytes>]`. SharpDPAPI is a C# port of some DPAPI functionality from @gentilkiwi's Mimikatz project.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Post-Exploitation, use SharpDPAPI because SharpDPAPI is a C# port of some DPAPI functionality from @gentilkiwi's Mimikatz project.
When the engagement is in the post_exploit phase, use SharpDPAPI as a cataloged Post-Exploitation tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `SharpDPAPI.exe search /type:registry [/path:HKLM\path\to\key] [/showErrors]` as documented upstream for SharpDPAPI.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `SharpDPAPI.exe search /type:folder /path:C:\path\to\folder [/maxBytes:<numOfBytes>] [/showErrors]` as documented upstream for SharpDPAPI.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `SharpDPAPI.exe search /type:file /path:C:\path\to\file [/maxBytes:<numOfBytes>]` as documented upstream for SharpDPAPI.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `SharpDPAPI.exe search /type:base64 [/base:<base64 string>]` as documented upstream for SharpDPAPI.
When chaining Post-Exploitation tools, pair SharpDPAPI with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `SharpDPAPI --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
